description: Port rich system prompt architecture from JS Copilot CLI to Python CLI — identity, guidelines, code change rules, memory injection, context compaction, custom instructions, and mode-specific messages.

userStories.title: Core system prompt builder with all static sections
userStories.description: As a developer using mARCH CLI, I want the AI to receive a rich, structured system prompt with identity, task guidelines, code change instructions, linting/testing rules, style rules, ecosystem tools, tips, and prohibited actions sections — matching the quality of the original JS Copilot CLI.
implementationNotes: Create new module `src/mARCH/core/prompts.py` with a `PromptBuilder` class.
Add an `AgentRole` enum (TASK_AGENT, CODING_AGENT) to `prompts.py` to support role-variant identity prompts.
Implement `PromptBuilder.build_system_prompt(name, role, context, **kwargs) -> str` as the main entry point.

Implement private methods for each section: `_identity_section(name, role)`, `_task_guidelines_section()`, `_code_change_section()`, `_linting_testing_section()`, `_style_section()`, `_ecosystem_tools_section()`, `_tips_section()`, `_prohibited_actions_section()`.
Each section method returns a string. The builder concatenates them with double-newline separators.
Define all prompt text as module-level string constants (e.g., `_TASK_GUIDELINES`, `_CODE_CHANGE_INSTRUCTIONS`) for easy testing and modification.

The identity section uses f-string interpolation: for TASK_AGENT role, agentName='Task Agent' with research/analysis skills; for CODING_AGENT role, agentName='Coding Agent' with coding skills. See JS lines 1327-1331.

Update `Agent._get_system_prompt()` in `src/mARCH/core/agent_state.py` to delegate to `PromptBuilder.build_system_prompt()` instead of constructing its own string. Pass `self.name`, current directory, language, branch, and available files from the existing logic.
Add `role: AgentRole` field to `Agent.__init__()` with default `AgentRole.TASK_AGENT`.

Create test file `src/mARCH/tests/test_prompts.py` with tests: (1) builder returns non-empty string, (2) identity section includes agent name and role-specific skills, (3) each section constant is non-empty, (4) TASK_AGENT vs CODING_AGENT produce different identity text, (5) build_system_prompt includes all sections.

Files touched: `src/mARCH/core/prompts.py` (new), `src/mARCH/core/agent_state.py` (edit _get_system_prompt and __init__), `src/mARCH/core/__init__.py` (export PromptBuilder, AgentRole), `src/mARCH/tests/test_prompts.py` (new).
acceptanceCriteria: A new file `src/mARCH/core/prompts.py` exists with `PromptBuilder` class and `AgentRole` enum.
`Agent._get_system_prompt()` delegates to `PromptBuilder.build_system_prompt()` and returns a string containing all 8 static sections.
The returned prompt includes role-variant identity text (different for TASK_AGENT vs CODING_AGENT).
The returned prompt includes task guidelines mentioning `<plan>` tags.
The returned prompt includes prohibited actions section with security rules.
All existing tests that call `Agent` or `get_conversation_context` continue to pass without modification.
New test file `src/mARCH/tests/test_prompts.py` has at least 5 passing tests.
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: Environment context and custom instructions
userStories.description: As a developer using mARCH CLI, I want the system prompt to include structured environment context (OS, git repo, available tools) and support reading custom instructions from `.github/copilot-instructions.md` so the AI understands my project conventions.
implementationNotes: Create `src/mARCH/core/custom_instructions.py` with a `CustomInstructionsLoader` class.
`CustomInstructionsLoader.load(repo_root: str | Path) -> str | None` reads `.github/copilot-instructions.md` from the repo root. Returns content string or None if file doesn't exist.

Add `_environment_context_section(context: AgentContext) -> str` to `PromptBuilder` in `prompts.py`. Include: current working directory, git repo root, git branch, OS (via `platform.system()`), and a note 'You do not need to make additional tool calls to verify this.'

Add `_custom_instructions_section(repo_root: str | None) -> str | None` to `PromptBuilder`. Uses `CustomInstructionsLoader.load()`. Returns None if no file found. When present, wraps content in a clear header section.
Update `PromptBuilder.build_system_prompt()` to accept optional `repo_root` kwarg and call both new sections.
Update `Agent._get_system_prompt()` to pass `repo_root` (derived from `self.context.current_directory` or git root detection).

Add tests to `src/mARCH/tests/test_prompts.py`: (1) environment section includes OS name and directory, (2) custom instructions returns None when file missing, (3) custom instructions returns content when file exists (use tmp_path fixture), (4) full prompt includes environment section.
Files touched: `src/mARCH/core/custom_instructions.py` (new), `src/mARCH/core/prompts.py` (edit), `src/mARCH/core/agent_state.py` (edit), `src/mARCH/tests/test_prompts.py` (edit).
acceptanceCriteria: A new file `src/mARCH/core/custom_instructions.py` exists with `CustomInstructionsLoader` class.
`PromptBuilder.build_system_prompt()` output includes an environment context section with OS and directory info.
When `.github/copilot-instructions.md` exists in the repo root, its content is included in the system prompt.
When the file does not exist, no custom instructions section appears (no error raised).
At least 3 new tests added for environment context and custom instructions.
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: Memory system prompt injection
userStories.description: As a developer using mARCH CLI, I want stored memories from previous sessions to be injected into the system prompt with guidance on how the AI should use, verify, and refresh them.
implementationNotes: Add `_memory_section(memories: list[dict] | None) -> str | None` to `PromptBuilder` in `prompts.py`.
Each memory dict has keys: `fact` (str), `subject` (str), `citations` (str). Format each as a bullet point with citation.

When `memories` is non-empty, return the JS CLI's memory preamble: explain that memories may be obsolete/incorrect, instruct to verify via citations, and instruct to re-store useful facts to retain them longer.
When `memories` is None or empty, return None (section omitted).
Update `PromptBuilder.build_system_prompt()` to accept optional `memories` kwarg.
Update `Agent._get_system_prompt()` to accept and forward a `memories` parameter (default None).

Add tests: (1) memory section returns None when memories is empty/None, (2) memory section includes preamble text when memories provided, (3) memory section includes each fact and citation, (4) full prompt omits memory section when no memories.
Files touched: `src/mARCH/core/prompts.py` (edit), `src/mARCH/core/agent_state.py` (edit), `src/mARCH/tests/test_prompts.py` (edit).
acceptanceCriteria: When memories list is provided, the system prompt includes a memory context section with the preamble about verifying and refreshing memories.
Each memory's fact and citation appear in the prompt.
When no memories are provided, the memory section is entirely absent from the prompt.
At least 3 new tests cover memory injection logic.
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: Context compaction and summarization
userStories.description: As a developer using mARCH CLI, I want the conversation history to be automatically compacted when the context window fills up, replacing old messages with an AI-generated summary so the session can continue without losing important context.
implementationNotes: Create `src/mARCH/core/context_compaction.py` with a `ContextCompactor` class.
Implement `should_compact(messages: list[dict], max_tokens: int = 100000) -> bool` — estimate token count (rough: 1 token ≈ 4 chars) and return True if over threshold (e.g., 75% of max_tokens).

Implement `build_summarization_prompt() -> str` — returns the JS CLI's structured summarization instruction asking the AI to produce a `<summary>` with: Task Overview, Current State, Important Discoveries, Next Steps, Context to Preserve.

Implement `build_compacted_messages(summary: str, original_user_messages: list[str]) -> list[dict]` — returns a new message list starting with a system-injected message in the format: 'The conversation history has been summarized...' followed by user messages and the summary in `<summary>` tags.

Implement async `compact(messages: list[dict], ai_complete_fn) -> list[dict]` — calls the AI with the summarization prompt appended to current messages, extracts `<summary>` from response, then calls `build_compacted_messages()`.
Integrate with `ConversationHistory` in `agent_state.py`: add a `compact_if_needed(ai_complete_fn, max_tokens)` method that checks and performs compaction.

Add test file `src/mARCH/tests/test_context_compaction.py` with tests: (1) should_compact returns False for short conversations, (2) should_compact returns True when over threshold, (3) build_summarization_prompt includes all 5 required sections, (4) build_compacted_messages produces correct format with summary tags, (5) compact extracts summary from mock AI response.
Files touched: `src/mARCH/core/context_compaction.py` (new), `src/mARCH/core/agent_state.py` (edit ConversationHistory), `src/mARCH/tests/test_context_compaction.py` (new).
acceptanceCriteria: A new file `src/mARCH/core/context_compaction.py` exists with `ContextCompactor` class.
`should_compact()` correctly detects when conversation exceeds token threshold.
`build_summarization_prompt()` returns a prompt that asks for Task Overview, Current State, Important Discoveries, Next Steps, and Context to Preserve sections.
`build_compacted_messages()` produces messages with the compacted history format including `<summary>` tags.
`ConversationHistory` has a `compact_if_needed` method.
At least 5 tests in `src/mARCH/tests/test_context_compaction.py`.
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures

userStories.title: Autopilot mode context injection and utility messages
userStories.description: As a developer using mARCH CLI, I want the correct mode-specific context message injected when transitioning between interactive and autopilot modes, and I want utility messages (quota insufficient, task not complete) available as prompt templates.
implementationNotes: Add mode transition message templates to `prompts.py` as module-level constants: `AUTOPILOT_MODE_MESSAGE`, `AUTOPILOT_FLEET_MODE_MESSAGE`, `INTERACTIVE_MODE_MESSAGE`, `INTERACTIVE_NO_EXEC_MESSAGE`.
Add `get_mode_transition_message(mode: ExecutionMode) -> str` function to `prompts.py` that returns the appropriate message for each mode.
Add `QUOTA_INSUFFICIENT_MESSAGE` and `TASK_NOT_COMPLETE_MESSAGE` constants to `prompts.py`.
Add `get_quota_message() -> str` and `get_task_not_complete_message() -> str` utility functions.
Update `ModeManager` in `execution_mode.py` to expose a `get_transition_context(new_mode: ExecutionMode) -> str` method that returns `get_mode_transition_message(new_mode)`.

Add tests: (1) each mode returns a distinct non-empty message, (2) AUTOPILOT message mentions auto-approved, (3) INTERACTIVE message mentions manual approval, (4) quota message mentions insufficient, (5) task_not_complete message mentions not yet marked complete.
Files touched: `src/mARCH/core/prompts.py` (edit), `src/mARCH/core/execution_mode.py` (edit), `src/mARCH/tests/test_prompts.py` (edit).
acceptanceCriteria: Mode transition constants exist in `prompts.py` for all 4 mode variants.
`get_mode_transition_message()` returns the correct message for AUTOPILOT, AUTOPILOT_FLEET, INTERACTIVE, and PLAN modes.
Quota and task-not-complete message templates are available as public constants/functions.
`ModeManager.get_transition_context()` returns the mode transition message.
At least 4 new tests cover mode messages and utility messages.
ruff check passes with no errors
pytest passes with no failures
